description: Adapter mapping for GPT-NeoX models (EleutherAI Pythia family, GPT-NeoX-20B). GPT-NeoX differs from Llama in several structural ways: (1) fused QKV projection (single query_key_value module instead of separate q/k/v), (2) parallel attention+MLP (attention and MLP computed in parallel, not sequentially), (3) different module naming (gpt_neox.layers instead of model.layers), (4) dense_h_to_4h / dense_4h_to_h instead of gate/up/down projections (no SwiGLU — uses GELU).

parameterized_indices.{i}: Layer index (0-based). Expands to all layers in the model (0..num_layers-1).

module_mappings.fused_description: GPT-NeoX fuses Q, K, V into a single linear projection (query_key_value). This single module maps to all three canonical projection names. The output tensor has shape [batch, seq_len, 3 * hidden_dim] and must be split along the last dimension to extract individual Q, K, V tensors. When inspecting q_proj, k_proj, or v_proj individually, the adapter slices the fused output.

module_mappings.note: GPT-NeoX uses GELU activation, not SwiGLU. There is no separate gate projection. dense_h_to_4h maps to up_proj as the primary expansion projection.

virtual_probes.capture_strategy: Captured from attention computation output. Requires eager SDPA.
Virtual for GPT-NeoX. Extracted by slicing the fused query_key_value output tensor along the last dimension: tensor[..., hidden_dim:2*hidden_dim].
Virtual for GPT-NeoX. Extracted by slicing the fused query_key_value output tensor along the last dimension: tensor[..., 2*hidden_dim:3*hidden_dim].
Captured as the input tensor to the transformer block (pre-hook on gpt_neox.layers.{i}).

In GPT-NeoX with parallel attention+MLP, residual_mid is captured after the input layernorm but before both attention and MLP (since they run in parallel). This differs from sequential architectures where residual_mid is between attention and MLP.
Captured as the output tensor of the transformer block (post-hook on gpt_neox.layers.{i}). This is the sum of residual + attention_output + mlp_output.

absent_components.reason: GPT-NeoX uses GELU activation, not SwiGLU. There is no gate projection. The single expansion linear (dense_h_to_4h) maps to up_proj.

notes: GPT-NeoX uses a fused QKV projection (query_key_value). The single module maps to q_proj, k_proj, and v_proj. The fused tensor has shape [batch, seq_len, 3 * hidden_dim] and is split to obtain individual projections.
GPT-NeoX computes attention and MLP in parallel (both receive the same layernorm output), unlike Llama which computes them sequentially. This affects residual_mid semantics.
GPT-NeoX uses LayerNorm (not RMSNorm), but both map to the same canonical ln1/ln2 names.
The absence of gate_proj is architecture-specific. Adapters SHOULD report absent canonical components so the daemon knows not to expect probes at those points.
GPT-NeoX uses 'embed_out' for the LM head (a separate linear layer), unlike Llama which uses 'lm_head'. Both map to the canonical 'lm_head' name.
Pythia models (70M through 12B) use the same GPT-NeoX architecture and module paths.
